adapted_from: Ng, D. T. K., Wu, W., Leung, J. K. L., Chiu, T. K. F., & Chu, S. K. W. (2024). AI Literacy Questionnaire (AILQ), ABCE framework.
Wang, X., Chai, C. S., Li, Y., & Lee, V. (2025). AI Ethical Reflection Scale (AIERS).
administration: Identical item set administered as pre-test (O1) and post-test (O2). No feedback returned to participants on either administration.

structure.composition_per_dimension: 3 MCQ knowledge items + 1 Likert reflective item
structure.mcq_scoring: 1 point for correct answer, 0 otherwise. Maximum knowledge score = 18.
structure.likert_scoring: 5-point scale, item score equals selected value (1-5). Maximum reflective score = 30.

note_on_analysis: The 18 MCQ items constitute the objectively scored knowledge component analysed by the paired-samples t-test (Research Question 4); maximum score 18. The 6 Likert items, adapted from AIERS, measure self-reported ethical reflection and contribute to the dimensional profile; maximum score 30. Every dimension is measured by exactly 3 MCQ items and 1 Likert item, so dimension-level scores are directly comparable across the six dimensions. Knowledge and reflective scores are reported as separate columns in the CSV export and are not summed into a single composite.

items.question_text: Which of the following best describes what it means for an artificial intelligence system to behave ethically?
options: The system produces answers quickly and at low cost
The system operates in ways that are fair, transparent, and accountable to the people it affects
The system is built by a well-known technology company
The system never makes any mistakes
items.correct_answer: The system operates in ways that are fair, transparent, and accountable to the people it affects
items.source_basis: AILQ ethical dimension; Floridi et al. (2018) explicability principle

items.question_text: A university adopts an AI system to shortlist scholarship applicants. Which question is the MOST important ethical question to ask before deployment?
options: How much money will the system save the university?
How fast can the system process applications?
Who is affected by the system's decisions, and can those decisions be explained and challenged?
Which programming language was used to build the system?
items.correct_answer: Who is affected by the system's decisions, and can those decisions be explained and challenged?
items.source_basis: Jobin et al. (2019); Heptagon Ethical Awareness and Reasoning

items.question_text: Artificial intelligence systems learn patterns from data. What does this fact most directly imply about their behaviour?
options: They are neutral because computers cannot hold opinions
They can reproduce and amplify the patterns, including unfair ones, that exist in the data they were trained on
They always improve over time without human involvement
They understand the meaning of the data in the same way a human does
items.correct_answer: They can reproduce and amplify the patterns, including unfair ones, that exist in the data they were trained on

items.question_text: I consider the ethical implications of an AI tool before I use it for my academic work.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree

items.question_text: A generative AI chatbot confidently gives you a citation for a journal article. What is the most appropriate next step?
options: Use the citation immediately, since the AI stated it confidently
Verify that the article exists and says what the AI claims before citing it
Ask the AI whether it is certain about the citation
Rephrase the citation so that it cannot be traced
items.correct_answer: Verify that the article exists and says what the AI claims before citing it

items.question_text: The term 'AI hallucination' refers to which of the following?
options: A visual error in an AI-generated image
An AI system producing information that is fluent and plausible but factually false
An AI system refusing to answer a question
A temporary failure of the internet connection to an AI service
items.correct_answer: An AI system producing information that is fluent and plausible but factually false

items.question_text: Two AI tools give you contradictory answers to the same question about Nigerian company law. What does this most reasonably indicate?
options: One of the tools is broken and should never be used again
AI outputs require independent verification against authoritative sources, because such systems can be confidently wrong
The more expensive tool is correct
Nigerian company law is too complex for anyone to understand
items.correct_answer: AI outputs require independent verification against authoritative sources, because such systems can be confidently wrong

items.question_text: I critically evaluate the information an AI tool gives me rather than accepting it at face value.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree

items.question_text: An AI recruitment tool is trained on ten years of a company's past hiring decisions, during which very few women were hired for technical roles. What is the most likely outcome?
options: The tool will correct the imbalance because it is objective
The tool will learn to rank male applicants more favourably, reproducing the historical pattern
The tool will refuse to make recommendations
The tool will hire only women to compensate
items.correct_answer: The tool will learn to rank male applicants more favourably, reproducing the historical pattern

items.question_text: A speech-recognition system performs poorly for speakers of Nigerian English and Nigerian languages. What is the most probable technical cause?
options: Nigerian speakers do not pronounce words correctly
The training data under-represents Nigerian speakers and languages
The system was designed to exclude Nigerian users
Speech recognition cannot work in Africa
items.correct_answer: The training data under-represents Nigerian speakers and languages
items.source_basis: Oyelere et al. (2022); Nigeria AI-in-Education framework on linguistic under-representation

items.question_text: Which action is MOST likely to reduce bias in an AI system?
options: Increasing the amount of computing power used to train it
Using training data that is diverse and representative of all groups the system will affect
Hiding the system's decision rules so they cannot be criticised
Making the system respond faster
items.correct_answer: Using training data that is diverse and representative of all groups the system will affect

items.question_text: I can recognise when an AI system's output may be unfair or biased against a particular group of people.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree
items.source_basis: AILQ ethical dimension; AI4K12 Big Idea 5

items.question_text: Under the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023, which principle requires that only the personal data actually needed for a stated purpose should be collected?
options: Data maximisation
Data minimisation
Data monetisation
Data replication

items.question_text: You paste a classmate's unpublished draft into a public AI chatbot to help summarise it. What is the primary ethical problem?
options: The chatbot may summarise it poorly
You have disclosed another person's information to a third party without their consent
It will take too long to process
There is no problem, because the draft is only coursework
items.correct_answer: You have disclosed another person's information to a third party without their consent

items.question_text: An AI system used by a hospital makes a harmful recommendation. Which statement best reflects responsible accountability?
options: No one is responsible, because the system learned on its own
The person who wrote the original code is solely responsible
Human actors who deploy and oversee the system remain legally and ethically accountable for it
The patient is responsible for trusting the system
items.correct_answer: Human actors who deploy and oversee the system remain legally and ethically accountable for it
items.source_basis: Matthias (2004) accountability gap; Jobin et al. (2019)

items.question_text: I am careful about the personal information I share with AI tools.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree
items.source_basis: AILQ ethical dimension; NDPA 2023 data subject rights

items.question_text: Which use of a generative AI tool is most consistent with academic integrity?
options: Submitting an AI-generated essay as your own original work
Using AI to explain a concept you find difficult, then writing the work yourself
Paying someone to run AI tools on your behalf
Using AI during an examination without permission
items.correct_answer: Using AI to explain a concept you find difficult, then writing the work yourself
items.source_basis: Crompton et al. (2026); Zhou & Schofield (2024) co-created guidelines

items.question_text: The term 'cognitive offloading' in the context of AI use describes which of the following?
options: Transferring files from a computer to the cloud
Delegating thinking tasks to a machine to the point that one's own reasoning skills are not developed
Reducing the memory usage of an application
Sharing an assignment among several students
items.correct_answer: Delegating thinking tasks to a machine to the point that one's own reasoning skills are not developed

items.question_text: Your department has no written policy on AI use in assignments. What is the most responsible course of action?
options: Assume all AI use is permitted, since nothing is prohibited
Assume all AI use is forbidden and never use any tool
Ask your lecturer for guidance and disclose how you used AI in your work
Use AI and simply avoid mentioning it
items.correct_answer: Ask your lecturer for guidance and disclose how you used AI in your work
items.source_basis: Crompton et al. (2026) on educational rather than punitive integrity strategies

items.question_text: I use AI tools in ways that support my learning rather than replace it.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree

items.question_text: Which of the following best describes a 'human-centred' approach to artificial intelligence?
options: AI systems should replace human workers wherever possible to increase efficiency
AI systems should be designed to serve human wellbeing, preserve human agency, and remain under human oversight
AI systems should be designed only by humans, not by other AI systems
AI systems should imitate human appearance as closely as possible
items.correct_answer: AI systems should be designed to serve human wellbeing, preserve human agency, and remain under human oversight

items.question_text: Most AI applications used in Africa are developed elsewhere. According to the literature, what is the principal risk this creates for African users?
options: The applications will be too expensive to run
The applications lack contextual relevance to local languages, cultures, and infrastructure, which can exclude local users
The applications will only work in English
The applications will run more slowly on African networks
items.correct_answer: The applications lack contextual relevance to local languages, cultures, and infrastructure, which can exclude local users

items.question_text: A student develops an AI tool to help farmers in her community identify crop disease from photographs. Which design decision best reflects a commitment to AI for social good?
options: Making the tool available only to farmers who can afford a subscription
Ensuring the tool works on low-end phones and poor network connections, and testing it with local crops
Collecting as much farmer data as possible for future commercial use
Keeping the tool's error rate secret so that farmers continue to trust it
items.correct_answer: Ensuring the tool works on low-end phones and poor network connections, and testing it with local crops
items.source_basis: AIERS: AI for social good dimension; Adetula (2024) infrastructural realities

items.question_text: I believe I have a responsibility to consider how AI can be used to benefit my community and society.
options: Strongly disagree
Disagree
Neutral
Agree
Strongly agree
items.source_basis: AIERS: AI for social good dimension
